turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: Using RBV, this study developed an empirical model to investigate the new roles of IT capabilities dimensions (i.e., physical IT infrastructure) in facilitating digital transformation in hospitals. This study focused on hospitals as predominantly major healthcare institutions. Indeed, hospitals are information-intensive, increasingly driving the implementation of digitization strategies, and should therefore be a research focus for digital transformation (Rockmann et al., 2015).

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Revised: This study adopts the Resource-Based View (RBV) framework to develop and test an empirical model that investigates the impact of physical IT infrastructure on digital transformation in hospitals. The research focuses on hospitals as crucial healthcare institutions that heavily rely on information technology. Given the importance of digitalization in the healthcare industry, the study aims to shed light on the key factors that facilitate successful digital transformation in hospitals.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: Using RBV, this study developed an empirical model to investigate the new roles of IT capabilities dimensions (including physical IT infrastructure) in facilitating digital transformation in hospitals.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Revised: This study employs the Resource-Based View (RBV) approach to construct an empirical model that examines the novel roles of IT capabilities dimensions, particularly the physical IT infrastructure, in driving the digital transformation of hospitals. The research focuses on understanding how these capabilities can be leveraged to facilitate successful digitalization initiatives in healthcare institutions.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: This study focused on hospitals in Iran, motivated by the growing interest and adoption of digital solutions in Iranian healthcare in response to the Covid-19 pandemic. The pandemic has highlighted the need for efficient and effective digital tools in healthcare, leading to a surge in the adoption of telemedicine, electronic prescriptions, electronic health records (EHR) and other digital health solutions. For example, the Social Insurance Organization, which is one of the largest health insurance companies in Iran, announced that from April to the end of October 2022, 97% of prescriptions of insured patients of this company were issued electronically (Rahimimoghadam, 2022). Moreover, EHR have been implemented for a remarkable 98% of individuals in Iran (Rezaei, 2023). Despite the increasing interest and adoption of digital tools in Iran's healthcare, hospitals still face challenges in maintaining and equipping costly physical IT infrastructure to implement digital transformation. On the other hand, there is also evidence to suggest that some hospitals have struggled to implement digital transformation despite having a strong physical IT infrastructure. Consequently, it is essential for Iranian hospitals to gain a comprehensive understanding of the role and mechanisms of IT capabilities (including physical IT infrastructure) in supporting digital transformation.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Revised: This study is conducted in the context of Iranian hospitals, driven by the growing interest and implementation of digital solutions in response to the Covid-19 pandemic. The pandemic has highlighted the critical need for efficient and effective digital tools in healthcare, fueling the adoption of telemedicine, electronic prescriptions, electronic health records (EHRs), and other digital health solutions in Iran. For instance, the use of electronic prescriptions accounted for 97% of prescriptions issued for insured patients of the Social Insurance Organization, a leading healthcare insurance provider in Iran, from April to October 2022 (Rahimimoghadam, 2022). Additionally, an exceptional 98% of individuals in Iran have had their EHRs implemented (Rezaei, 2023). Despite the increasing interest and adoption of digital tools, Iranian hospitals still face challenges in acquiring and maintaining costly physical IT infrastructure. Moreover, some hospitals struggle to implement digital transformation effectively despite owning a strong physical IT infrastructure. Therefore, it is crucial for Iranian hospitals to investigate the roles and factors involved in IT capabilities, including physical IT infrastructure, to facilitate and support successful digital transformation.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: This study focused on hospitals in Iran, motivated by the growing interest and adoption of digital solutions in Iranian healthcare in response to the Covid-19 pandemic.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Revised: This study examines hospitals in Iran as the research context, spurred by the increasing interest and uptake of digital solutions in response to the Covid-19 pandemic. The pandemic has underscored the importance of effective and efficient digital tools in healthcare, accelerating the adoption of telemedicine, electronic prescriptions, electronic health records (EHRs), and other digital health solutions in Iran.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: Despite the increasing interest and adoption of digital tools in Iran's healthcare, hospitals still face challenges in maintaining and equipping costly physical IT infrastructure to implement digital transformation. On the other hand, there is also evidence to suggest that some hospitals have struggled to implement digital transformation despite having a strong physical IT infrastructure. Consequently, it is essential for Iranian hospitals to gain a comprehensive understanding of the role and mechanisms of IT capabilities (including physical IT infrastructure) in supporting digital transformation.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Revised: Although there has been a surge in the adoption of digital tools in Iranian healthcare, hospitals encounter obstacles in acquiring and maintaining expensive physical IT infrastructure necessary for implementing digital transformation successfully. Furthermore, despite possessing a robust physical IT infrastructure, some hospitals have faced difficulties in implementing digital transformation effectively. Therefore, it is crucial for Iranian hospitals to comprehend the mechanisms and functions of IT capabilities, including physical IT infrastructure, for supporting successful digital transformation.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: Despite the increasing interest and adoption of digital tools in Iran's healthcare, hospitals still face challenges in maintaining and equipping costly physical IT infrastructure to implement digital transformation, while mechanisms that this infrastructure influence DT, is still lacking. Because, despite possessing a robust physical IT infrastructure, some hospitals have faced difficulties in implementing digital transformation successfully. Therefore, it is crucial for Iranian hospitals to comprehend the mechanisms and roles of IT capabilities, including physical IT infrastructure, for supporting successful digital transformation.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Revised: Despite the growing interest and adoption of digital tools in Iranian healthcare, hospitals encounter challenges in procuring and maintaining expensive physical IT infrastructure required for implementing digital transformation. Additionally, the mechanisms through which this infrastructure influences digital transformation are still not well-understood. Even with a robust physical IT infrastructure, some hospitals have struggled to implement digital transformation effectively. As a result, it is imperative for Iranian hospitals to gain a comprehensive understanding of the mechanisms and functions of IT capabilities, including physical IT infrastructure, to support successful digital transformation.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: Despite the increasing interest and adoption of digital tools in Iran's healthcare, hospitals still face challenges in maintaining and equipping costly physical IT infrastructure to implement digital transformation, while the mechanisms through which this infrastructure influences digital transformation are still not well-understood. some hospitals have faced difficulties in implementing digital transformation successfully, despite possessing a robust physical IT infrastructure.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Revised: Although there has been a rise in interest and acceptance of digital tools in Iranian healthcare, hospitals continue to experience difficulties in acquiring and equipping expensive physical IT infrastructure required for implementing digital transformation. Additionally, the mechanisms through which this infrastructure influences digital transformation remain insufficiently understood. Despite possessing a strong physical IT infrastructure, some hospitals have struggled to carry out digital transformation effectively, indicating the need for a more in-depth understanding of these mechanisms.
